entry.title: Re: GST Margin scheme on second hand material purchases
entry.content: There should be some logic to arrive this margin scheme.  So have another condition type to capture the margin scheme value before tax condition type.  Depending upon the logic, maintain the condition record.

entry.title: Re: Interact programmatically with SAP CAP framework's No...
entry.content:
The cds bind command was the missing piece! Thanks Gregor!

Using it, profile selection and binding resolution don't have to be part of my script logic anymore. Only had to change how I call my script, not from within my subfolder anymore, but from my CAP modules' root folder.

# Execute in CAP module root (where your package.json is)
cds bind --profile hybrid-dev --exec -- node migration/cap-startup-test2.js
//file: migration/cap-startup-test2.js
const cds = require('@sap/cds')

async function capStartup() {
const app = cds.app
cds.emit('bootstrap', app)

// load model from all sources
const csn = await cds.load('*').then(cds.minify)
cds.model = cds.compile.for.nodejs(csn)
cds.emit('loaded', cds.model)

// connect to prominent required services
if (cds.requires.db) cds.db = await cds.connect.to('db')
if (cds.requires.messaging) await cds.connect.to('messaging')

// serve own services as declared in model
cds.serve('all').from(csn).in(app)
await cds.emit('served', cds.services)
}

async function main() {
// Startup CAP Framework
await capStartup()

// Do your thing
const { MyEntity } = cds.db.entities('my.namespace')
console.log(await SELECT.from(MyEntity).columns('count(ID) as cnt'))

// Shutdown CAP Framework
cds.exit()
}

main()

entry.title: Re: Duplicate Inspection lot creation on Sales Return Ins...
entry.content:
Next check: did somebody move the 08 inspection type to another origin? SPRO>Quality Management>Quality inspection>Maintain Inspection Lot Origins and Assign Inspection Types

Origin 05 standard looks like this:

entry.title: Re: How do I trigger a User Defined Object in PostTransactionNotice
entry.content:
Hi,

I'd like to share an example of a query for postransactionnotice I have ever made on April 18, 2012 ago.

/* customized date setting in UDF day name, dd-mmm-yyyy */
-- result example : Wednesday, 18-May-2012
If @object_type = '67' and @transaction_type in ('A','U')

Begin

declare @day as varchar(30)
declare @date1 as varchar(30)
declare @Hari as nvarchar(30)
set @day = (SELECT distinct
case
when DATENAME(dw, (T0.[DocDate])) = 'Monday' then cast('Monday' as nvarchar(20))
when DATENAME(dw, (T0.[DocDate])) = 'Tuesday' then cast('Tuesday' as nvarchar(20))
when DATENAME(dw, (T0.[DocDate])) = 'Wednesday' then cast('Wednesday' as nvarchar(20))
when DATENAME(dw, (T0.[DocDate])) = 'Thursday' then cast('Thursday' as nvarchar(20))
when DATENAME(dw, (T0.[DocDate])) = 'Friday' then cast('Friday' as nvarchar(20))
when DATENAME(dw, (T0.[DocDate])) = 'Saturday' then cast('Saturday' as nvarchar(20))
when DATENAME(dw, (T0.[DocDate])) = 'Sunday' then cast('Sunday' as nvarchar(20))
end from OWTR t0 where t0.Docentry = @List_of_cols_val_tab_del)
set @date1 = (select distinct
cast(day(a.[DocDate]) as nvarchar(20))+'-'+case
when month(a.docdate) = '1' then cast('Jan' as nvarchar(20))
when month(a.docdate) = '2' then cast('Feb' as nvarchar(20))
when month(a.docdate) = '3' then cast('Mar' as nvarchar(20))
when month(a.docdate) = '4' then cast('Apr' as nvarchar(20))
when month(a.docdate) = '5' then cast('May' as nvarchar(20))
when month(a.docdate) = '6' then cast('Jun' as nvarchar(20))
when month(a.docdate) = '7' then cast('Jul' as nvarchar(20))
when month(a.docdate) = '8' then cast('Aug' as nvarchar(20))
when month(a.docdate) = '9' then cast('Sep' as nvarchar(20))
when month(a.docdate) = '10' then cast('Oct' as nvarchar(20))
when month(a.docdate) = '11' then cast('Nov' as nvarchar(20))
when month(a.docdate) = '12' then cast('Dec' as nvarchar(20))
end+'-'+ cast(year(a.[DocDate]) as nvarchar(20))
FROM Owtr a WHERE a.docentry = @List_of_cols_val_tab_del)

update OWTR
set U_SI_HARI_TGL_KIRIM = (@day+','+' '+@date1)
where @object_type = '67'
and DocEntry = @List_of_cols_val_tab_del

end

 

so, the postransactionnotice works when I add or update the transfer document. So, you must make sure that you use a valid document to update the custom tables.

if not, it won't works.

 

Rgds,

Jimmy

entry.title: Re: hdbcli seems to have a syntax error during connection
entry.content:
Kerberos is used when only the username is specified:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_HANA_CLIENT/f1b440ded6144a54ada97ff95dac7adf/b250e7fef8614ea0a0973d58eb73bda8.html#user-ids-and-passwords 

Is that what does not work for you?

entry.title: Re: Firfeighter log sessions are not ending after ID logs...
entry.content:
Hello @pravallika90,

 

From my experience, it is directly connected with changes introduced with SAP Access Control 12.0 SP21 for Firefighter logging - to make it a long story short: FF users should click on this new button available Logoff to close the session.

It is quite nice described in the following blog from @Mate: https://community.sap.com/t5/financial-management-blogs-by-sap/good-to-know-firefighter-session-related-changes-from-access-control/ba-p/13575642

What I can suggest from my experience also is to check:

  • missing authorizations for both the Firefighter ID, Firefighter User and user on which you are running synchronization jobs (both on GRC side and backend system side)
  • missing authorization for RFC user (both on GRC side and backend system)
  • go through the package of SAP Notes mentioned in a blog from @Mate - you have to implement quite bunch of them to be confident that system is working as expected
  • open tcode GRAC_FFSESSION to validate the status of those open sessions
  • go through the SLG1 / SLGD logs for jobs (you can validate also if there are any dumps in ST22)

The outcome that you are experiencing might be a mix of those.

 

Regards,

Lukasz

entry.title: E-Learning for SAP Signavio Adoption Starter for Process Manager and Collaboration Hub is available!
entry.content:
Hello SAP Signavio community, 

SAP Signavio Value Map team is excited to announce we have released the next E-Learning from the SAP Signavio Adoption Starter series.  This E-learning covers the adoption of SAP Signavio Process Manager and Collaboration Hub. 

What is the session about? 

This e-learning course aims to give you a thorough understanding of how to effectively use the SAP Signavio Process Manager and Collaboration Hub for successful adoption and application. As you progress through the course, you'll receive guidance on configuring workspaces, using BPMN (Business Process Model and Notation), and exploring the tool's features, complete with demonstrations.

What are the session goals?

  • Discover how SAP Signavio can kickstart your organization's transformation journey using the SAP Signavio Process Manager and Collaboration Hub.
  • Gain an understanding of SAP Signavio Process Manager and Collaboration Hub, including its usage, navigation, and setting up initial configurations.
  • Develop the ability to create process models in SAP Signavio Process Manager using BPMN 2.0.
  • Get acquainted with other crucial SAP Signavio Solutions integral to your transformation efforts.

How to access? 

If you haven't yet signed up for the SAP Enterprise Support Academy and Value Maps, don't worry, we've got you covered! Get started with SAP Enterprise Support Academy and Value Maps here. (Check out our step-by-step instructional video   on how to join SAP Enterprise Support Academy and Value maps.)

Direct link to the new E-Learning session:

SAP Signavio Adoption Starter:  Process Manager and Collaboration Hub

Do you need additional help? 

Don't hesitate to get in touch with our team using the following form. (Please select the "SAP Signavio | Business Process Transformation" topic).

entry.title: Cross order staging process flow
entry.content:
There are two types of staging processes namely:

1)      Single order staging 

2)      Cross order staging 

 

1) Single Order Staging:

Single Order Staging is a logistics and supply chain management concept where the process of preparing and staging inventory for a single order is executed. This process involves gathering, organizing, and preparing the items specified in a single customer order before they are dispatched for delivery. Single Order Staging is particularly important for order fulfillment and ensures that each order is processed accurately and efficiently. In SAP Extended Warehouse Management (EWM), single-order staging allows users to request that components be staged for a specific order. This involves moving components from a warehouse to a production supply area (PSA).

 

2) Cross Order Staging:

Cross Order Staging is a logistics strategy used to optimize the handling and preparation of multiple orders simultaneously. Unlike Single Order Staging, which focuses on preparing one order at a time, Cross Order Staging involves organizing and staging items from multiple orders together in a way that can improve efficiency and reduce overall handling time. For cross-order staging, the system proposes a quantity for staging for each component that is required on the PSA. The system considers only PMRs with the status Released. You can create warehouse tasks for the replenishment with a different quantity to the quantity proposed by the system.

 

Process Order:

A Process Order is a term commonly used in manufacturing and production environments, particularly in industries that use process-based production methods, such as chemical, pharmaceutical, or food and beverage industries. It refers to a detailed plan or instruction set for producing a specific quantity of a product, typically involving multiple steps or stages in the production process.

 

PMR:

In SAP Extended Warehouse Management (SAP EWM), a production material request (PMR) is a document that contains information about the materials required for a manufacturing order. This information includes the products, quantities, production supply areas, and dates for which the materials will be staged. The PMR is created in SAP EWM after a manufacturing order is created and released in the ERP system. The PMR acts as a link between production planning and extended warehouse management, and is essential for efficient production.

 

Staging:

Staging refers to the process of preparing and organizing goods or materials in a designated area or staging location within a warehouse before they are either shipped out to customers, used in production, or moved to another location within the warehouse. Staging is a crucial step in warehouse management and inventory handling as it helps to streamline operations, improve efficiency, and ensure that goods are ready for the next phase in the supply chain.

 

Cross Order Staging Process Flow:

Process Order:

Create the Process Order using the transaction code COR1

Provide the below details and save the Process order:

a)      Materials

b)      Basic dates (start and end)

 

PMR:

Navigate to T-code /N/SCWM/PMR to open Maintain Prd Material Request.

Click the drop down in Find and select Manufacturing order.

Enter Process Order.

Click the Execute button.

PMR Document will be displayed.

Verify PMR for Process Orders- Item and header details for correctness like staging method, product, qty, bin, PSA, Process type for Single order staging

 

Staging:

Navigate to /N/SCWM/STAGE to schedule staging for production

Provide the below details:

1)      Warehouse number

2)      Staging method

3)      Manufacturing Order

Deselect Test mode check box and then click on the execute button.

Five warehouse tasks are created.

 

 

Navigate to the transaction code /SCWM/MON and then go to Documents. Warehouse tasks

Five warehouse tasks are Confirmed in the Background.

 

Conclusion:

The Process flow steps for Cross Order Staging is provided.

entry.title: Re: RAP add table in ObjectPage
entry.content: My question is more general. I want to display an associated CDS view as a table in the ObjectPage.

entry.title: Re: RAP add table in ObjectPage
entry.content: if that is the case, your target cds needs to have lineitem annotation.

entry.title: Override positionCriticality field in SuccessFactors using an integration (BTP) from HCM
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

we are using the field positionCriticality in SuccessFactors Succession. This field is a position data which will update by the standard integration via BTP from HCM. Unfortunately the field gets override every day. Is there a possibility to adjust the integration that the content of the field will not override in SuccessFactors. Is it true, that there is only the following solution possible: 

Create a customer-specific field in SAP HCM, have it maintained there and include it in the “delta migration” of the OM objects

Does anybody see an alternative for this issue?

Thanks a lot for supporting! BR, Sandra

entry.title: Re: Offboarding Triggers
entry.content: Hi @mendozacm, did you ever get a response to your question?

entry.title: Re: 3 Decimal places for Data Migration in S4 Public cloud
entry.content:
Hello Dineshkumar,

for which objects do have this problem? Only for Open items AP or also for AR and G/L open items?

BR Frank

entry.title: SAP SuccessFactors Story Reports: apply conditional formatting table dimension
entry.content:
Hi, 

I have seen that it is possible to highlight information in the charts of story reports. However, I have not found a way to do the same for a table dimension. Although there is the option in Tools > Conditional formatting, despite assigning the colors, no changes are visible in the table.

Has anyone managed to highlight a table column according to the answers obtained?

entry.title: Re: how to handle too much output template requirement in...
entry.content:
Hi Feng,

What types of difference do you anticipate between the templates?

Is it the logo, title or is it the layout that changes - perhaps you could describe a few examples.

In output parameter determination you can determine the template based on BP receiver but that requires a separate template, which doesn't seem to be what you want.

The other option is to define a separate master template. Or you could potentially do something in the scripting logic of the form.

If none of those are options, you can consider a side by side in BTP using the adobe forms service. Similar to https://extensibilityexplorer.cfapps.eu10.hana.ondemand.com/ExtensibilityExplorer/#/ExtScenario/2401

Thank you

Jerry

entry.title: Override positionCriticality field in SuccessFactors using an integration (BTP) from HCM
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

we are using the field positionCriticality in SuccessFactors Succession. This field is a position data which will update by the standard integration via BTP from HCM. Unfortunately the field gets override every day. Is there a possibility to adjust the integration that the content of the field will not override in SuccessFactors. Is it true, that there is only the following solution possible: 

 

Create a customer-specific field in SAP HCM, have it maintained there and include it in the “delta migration” of the OM objects.

Does anybody see an alternative for this issue?

Thanks a lot for supporting! BR, Sandra

entry.title: Re: How to perform modifications in Standard Migration Object with Migration Object Modeler App
entry.content:
Hi Moumita,

just a small comment regarding the term "modification" in the title. At SAP modifications are normally  changes on exiting report, programs and so on, that are causing issues later during upgrades. If you are following the SAP Note 2999428 - Collective Note for extensions via Model Your Migration Objects - Migration Object Modeler App in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition (Migrate Data Using Staging Tables), you are adding additional standard fields to the object. That change is update-safe. It will not cause any issues as the object is not in state "modified". The object will still receive updates.

BR

Frank

entry.title: How to maintain the Long Text Template Language for Maintenance Request based on Logon Language?
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

I want to have the long text template to be displayed in the users logon language in the Create Maintenance Request App (F1511A). 

There is a nice and informative blog post in the community about the extensibility of it, but it does not have the information about the language of the long text template. You can read it here: Long Text Template Extensibility for Maintenance Request 

For reference I am attaching the image where in one system the logon language takes effect in the long text template but in another the contents on the page is affected by logon language but not the long text template.

 

Anyone who can give some pointers or way to achieve the solution, I thank you in advance.

entry.title: Return to supplier process in case of replaced Goods
entry.content:
Dear SAP Experts,

In case of supplier return with PO Return, if the supplier propose to replace the items returned with new ones which have differents caractéristiques (Batch, Shelf Life..etc) How do I integrate those matrials 

 

1- Create PO QTY = 300, Good receipt
2- PO Return  with Qty 100, Good issue  MVT TYPE 161
3- if the supplier raplace the 100 quantity with new ones (New bathes & Caracteristics), how to enter those materials in the system ?

entry.title: Re: Automatic Synchronization not working in SAP Service...
entry.content: Thanks for the help.

entry.title: Re: Save Data from a CSV file
entry.content: yes it's solved

entry.title: Re: Handling Unit of Measurements in Data Migration cockpit.
entry.content:
The correct ISOCode for hours is "HUR" that is assigned to internal UOM "STD". Normally an application should use STD as internal UOM and not H.

BR

Frank

entry.title: Re: CAP - CDS REPL to connect to db {kind : hana}
entry.content: This is a strange problem. It appears to be an issue with encoding of the cds files. But, this would have been the case with serve as well. Perhaps your global cds is at fault. I am not familiar with yarn. When npm is used to run a cds command, it uses local cds, if there is one. I am assuming yarn does the same. Try using local cds with node_modules/.bin/cds. Also, if you use cds (instead of cds-ts) you have to precompile typescript.

entry.title: Re: Duplicate Purchase Requisitions
entry.content:
Hi @Caetano , Thanks for the reply.
Also longer lead times maintained in the purchase info record. Here, we do have 168 days of Planned Delivery time in info record which is greater than the Planned Delivery time maintained in the MRP2 view of material master i.e., 1.

Also we are maintaining rescheduling horizon as 100 days at plant level. Planning horizon also as 100 days but hope this planning horizon does not have any impact on MRP Live.
Is our understanding correct that the issue is due to the longer lead time and rescheduling horizon?

entry.title: Update your Public Cloud Expertise - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Product Expert Training HY2 2024
entry.content:
We’re pleased to invite you to the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Product Expert Training, running from September 23rd to October 2nd. These sessions are a valuable opportunity to deepen your knowledge and sharpen your skills in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.  

We’ve packed this event with insights into the latest innovations, including a strong focus on AI and Intelligent Technology—areas that are rapidly becoming essential across all industries. Our product experts will also share updates across key ERP topics including Finance, manufacturing and  supply chain alongside industry specific insights technical cross topics that will help you better support customers and partners. Benefit from in-depth tutorials on the latest features with live system demos showing the hands-on user experience. 

Over these 10 days, you will have the opportunity to engage directly with our experts and peers and discuss real-world scenarios that mirror the challenges our customers face. This is a chance to not only learn but also to contribute your own ideas and experiences and we look forward to your engagement at the Q&A of each session. 

Mark your calendars and get ready to join us from September 23rd to October 2nd. This training is designed to give you the tools and insights you need to excel in your role and drive success with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. 

View all sessions on SAP Learning and select which webinars you wish to register for. Alternatively, you can register for specific LoB & End-to-End process topics via the tracks in the boxes below. By registering, you will receive a calendar blocker for the webinar under the topic with links to access the webinar directly.

For an overview of all webinars & scheduling, view the detailed agenda or check out our new landing page on SAP Learning.

 

Registration Instructions for Partners.

  • This webinar series is available for all partners, with a valid Universal ID to access SAP Learning platform AND a valid Partner S user ID to access PartnerEdge.com  
  • Make sure you are logged-in on the SAP learning platform to the view the sessions  
  • Log-in via the icon in the top right corner.

    Troubleshooting

    • If you have not obtained a Universal ID so far, please follow instructions on this link to obtain an Universal ID - https://partneredge.sap.com/en/partnership/manage/mmp/uid.html
    • If you have issue with your Partner S user ID – please write to partner@sap.com

       

      Sign-up Instructions for SAP Colleagues

      In order to sign up for the webinars, you will need to have an up to date SAP Learning Hub license Internal Edition. Make sure you are logged on to SAP Learning Hub to view the sessions

      If you do not have an active SAP Learning Hub license, renew your license here for the Internal Edition which is free of charge. Please click on “Access” in the link to get the license. After getting the license, you will be able to register with the link above. If you are still facing issues, follow the steps in our troubleshooting guide.

entry.title: Re: Link Sales Order & Purchase Order in SAP Standard Rep...
entry.content: Check if Report IW12 or RIBELF20 meets your requirements?

entry.title: TS-Based Supply Optimizer finishes before Maximum Solving Runtime with Technical Gap (1 iteration)
entry.content:
What could be the reason that a TS-Based Supply Optimizer finishes before Maximum Solving Runtime with a Technical Gap (Technical Gap created by product composition > 2.7)?

We monitor that there has only been one iteration of the Planning Run. Why did the run not create more iterations &/or a longer Optimizer solving time, since the results were sub-optimal (i.e. there was a Technical Gap)?

In our case, the Optimizer solving time was 2500 seconds, which is far below the Maximum Solving Runtime for Optimizer maintained as 15000.

entry.title: Re: Cloud Integration - Advanced Event Mesh Adapter Issue
entry.content:
Hi Vidyadha,

Thanks for the response.

I finally found the issue, it was due to the Adapter Version 1.0.0.

I have now deployed the latest version 1.3.0 and everything is working fine.

Messages still getting published, but the adapter does not get an error anymore.

Did not change anithing else, just deploying new adapter version.

Best regards.

Mikel

entry.title: ERMS inbound mail receivers
entry.content:
Hello,

For inbound mails, we need two different e-mails addresses (e.g. a@mydomain.com and b@mydomain.com), in order to have different people in charge of the specific user request. Company has  two customer service departments, in the same productive client, for this reason business needs different email destinations. As the SU01 user for SMTP connection (with profile S_AS.ASCON) is assigned the first address (a@mydomain.com), how can we manage the email sent by customer to the second address (b@mydomain.com) ?

Thanks in advance!

Chris

entry.title: Meas doc creation allows points for items not related to Notif Tech OBj
entry.content:
While maintaining a notification for a tech object, users are going to Extras/Measurement Docs.

They then enter a measurement point. The MP entered is not related to the Tech Object on the notification.

Is this proper behavior? Do we need to find a note (I have not found one) or use a badi to enforce Meas Points belong to the notification being maintained?

To expand your network, meet our SAP Champions and SAP Mentors.

entry.title: Re: Duplicate Purchase Requisitions
entry.content: Yes, the issue is related to the long lead time. Rescheduling check will not consider the lead time from the info record to calculate the replenishment lead time, so your PO is outside the rescheduling horizon, and that is why MRP does not consider it.

entry.title: Attach File Evidences in Data Collections and Non Conformances
entry.content:
Introduction

In this blog post, I will demonstrate how to attach file evidence when capturing data collections or raising non-conformances against an SFC (Shop Floor Control) within SAP Digital Manufacturing. This functionality is frequently required during quality inspection operations.

During quality inspections, workers often need to capture data collections or raise non-conformances against an SFC, and attaching files as evidence is a common requirement. SAP Digital Manufacturing provides built-in support for file attachments in both data collection and non-conformance processes.



Attach Files to Data Collection Parameters

In POD Designer, open your POD and select 'Data Collection' plugin. In the 'Configuration Panel', scroll down to find 'List Name' and click to browse.

In the list popup, click Details icon for the list in use.

Move the 'Attachments' column from left ('Available Columns') to right ('Assigned Columns') and click 'Save'.

From now on, any POD using the list that you changed (in this case, 'DEFAULT_DATA_ENTRY'), will have the 'Attachments' capability enabled.

Attachments can be visualized in SFC Report at 'Data Collection' tab, just click 'Show More per Row' and 'Preview' to see it.

See more about it here: Data Collections.

Attach Files to Non Conformances

In POD Designer, open your POD and select 'Nonconformance Data Entry' plugin. In the 'Configuration Panel', switch 'ON' the 'Enable Attachments' option.

From now on, 'Nonconformance Data Entry' plugin on that specific POD will show the 'Attachments' option.

Which can also be visualized in SFC Report, after clicking on a specific nonconformance in 'Nonconformance' tab to see 'Nonconformance Details' page.

* The file name must be up to 128 characters and the file size must not exceed 25 MB.

See more about it here: Nonconformance Data Entry

Conclusion

In this blog post, you learned how to attach file evidences to Data Collection parameters or Non Conformances, during the manufacturing process, against an SFC within SAP Digital Manufacturing, which is a very common requirement.

In order to do that, you added 'Attachments' column to Data Collection plugin list, or switched on 'Enable Attachments' option in Nonconformance Data Entry plugin in POD Designer. You also learned that attachments can be later on visualized in SFC Report app.

Do you like this post? Please let me know in the comments section what you think. Any feedback is highly appreciated.

Or, if you have any questions, please check SAP Community Q&A Area, or comment down below.

Thanks,
Manoel Costa

entry.title: Re: Real Talk with Stephanie and Konstantin | SAP Business Technology Platform Track Leads
entry.content: Excellent!

entry.title: Re: Can not create temp file: Failed to open the connection. Database Vendor Code: 17002
entry.content: Unfortunately, SAP tech support is not able to assist me with my issue and believe that it's a problem with my Linux environment.

entry.title: Re: Embed stored procedure in Calculation view in Hana
entry.content: As I know, you cannot directly consume a stored procedure in a Calculation view in SAP HANA. Calculation views are used for complex calculations, which are not possible with table functions or SQL script procedures.

entry.title: HCM Brasil – eSocial S-1.3 – Entrega dos eventos com escopo limitado
entry.content:
Boa tarde pessoal,

Na última sexta-feira, 30 de agosto, finalizamos a primeira etapa das nossas entregas referentes ao eSocial S-1.3.

Neste primeiro momento, focamos em entregar todas as modificações dos eventos de escopo limitado, proporcionando aos nossos clientes e parceiros mais tempo para adequar seus sistemas.

Nesta entrega, as seguintes SAP notes foram liberadas:

  • 3503143 - Prerequisite objects for SAP Note 3503194
  • 3503215 - eSocial S-2555 - Prerequisite transformation object for SAP Note 3503194
  • 3503194 - eSocial S-1.3 - S-2555 - Request of Taxes Information Resulting from Labor Lawsuit
  • 3504537 - eSocial - S-1.3 - Transformation objects for limited scope events without changes
  • 3506238 - Prerequisite objects for SAP Note 3506186
  • 3506147 - eSocial S-1.3 - Prerequisite transformation object for SAP Note 3506186
  • 3506186 - eSocial S-1.3 - Events with Limited Scope with Structure Changes

Também liberamos a nota que possibilita a mudança de layout no sistema:

  • 3501788 - eSocial - New S-1.3 option for layout configuration

Esta nota entrega a nova opção de customização S01030 que você pode configurar na view Valores de cliente para opções de configuração (V_T5F99K2).

Caso tenha alguma dúvida de como configurar, pode consultar nosso blogpost referente a versão S-1.2 que ensina neste link, só lembrando de utilizar a nova opção S1030.

IMPORTANTE:

É de extrema importância que, ao configurar uma nova versão, a configuração das versões prévias sejam mantidas inalteradas. Caso uma versão anterior seja excluída, o sistema irá gerar, dentre outros efeitos colaterais, eventos de exclusão e retificações indevidas, a depender do evento que você estiver gerando.
Por fim, recomendamos que a troca de versão seja realizada sempre ao final de uma competência, a fim de evitar que sejam gerados e enviados, dentro de um mesma competência, eventos com versões de leiautes diferentes.

Atualização do planejamento

Conforme divulgado neste blogpost, a SAP planeja entregar essa alteração legal em 3 etapas:

  • Etapa 1 em 30 de agosto de 2024: Eventos com escopo limitado (são aqueles que dependem exclusivamente de implementação de clientes e parceiros). ✅
  • Etapa 2 em 04 de outubro de 2024: Alterações relacionadas ao evento S-1210 - Pagamentos de Rendimentos do Trabalho. 
  • Etapa 3 em 01 de novembro de 2024: Alterações dos demais eventos cobertos pela solução standard.

Você encontra maiores informações sobre esse planejamento acessando o Regulatory Change Manager.

Lembrando que todas as entregas referentes ao eSocial você consegue encontrar na SAP Note 3418378 - eSocial - Overview - 2024.

Gostou desse post? Dê um Like e compartilhe o conteúdo com seus colegas.

Fiquem à vontade para deixar um feedback, comentário ou pergunta sobre este Blog post no espaço abaixo. E não esqueçam de seguir a tag HCM Payroll Brazil na SAP Community para ficarem ligados nas últimas notícias.

Um abraço,

Anderson Marques

entry.title: Re: Defect Tracking API data posted from CPI to S4 shown...
entry.content:
Looks not working as expected. Possible reasons could be:

1. Data Synchronization: Delay or problem with data sync between QMFE and TCODE QF21 due to a system glitch or software bug.
2. API Configuration: Incorrect API settings or parameters not reflecting changes in TCODE QF21.
3. Data Mapping: Issues with data mapping between the API and TCODE QF21 due to incorrect or missing rules.
4. Authorization: API unable to update TCODE QF21 due to insufficient permissions or access rights.
5. Software Bug: A coding error or design flaw causing the issue.

The best will be to open a support ticket.

entry.title: Re: how to handle too much output template requirement in Cloud
entry.content: Sounds like a terrible proposal that would require far too many resources... one must always consider ongoing maintenance and support beyond the act of building.

entry.title: Re: How can I see the TDS and GST reports plant-wise not...
entry.content: In standard SAP, the TDS and GST reports are usually generated based on the company code, not the plant code. May you have to use a workaround.

entry.title: Re: Defect Tracking API data posted from CPI to S4 shown...
entry.content:
Looks not working as expected. Possible reasons could be:

 

1. Data Synchronization: Delay or problem with data sync between QMFE and TCODE QF21 due to a system glitch or software bug.

2. API Configuration: Incorrect API settings or parameters not reflecting changes in TCODE QF21.

3. Data Mapping: Issues with data mapping between the API and TCODE QF21 due to incorrect or missing rules.

4. Authorization: API unable to update TCODE QF21 due to insufficient permissions or access rights.

5. Software Bug: A coding error or design flaw causing the issue.

 

The best will be to open a support ticket.

entry.title: No logs for successful messages in SAP CPI
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

In the message monitoring logs are not available for successful messages. We can only see that messages is successful.

For the errors we have logs, but for successful messages we don't have.

As the Retention period is 30 days, we can only see successful and failed messages. 

For eg. I would like to know File Transmission Logs.

File is picked from S4 AL11 - Archive - Target (These steps we can't see in case of INFO level and we can't change it to TRACE everytime).

Can anyone please help?

We have a requirement wherein we would like to remove mass key figure fixing. Can you please suggest a way how this can be done at a large scale?

Thank and Regards.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Override Key Figure Fixing (Mass Delete)
entry.content:
Hello,

please see the following Help portal page in the section on 'Mass Deletion of Fixing Information'.  

Regards,
Chris
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Refurbishment Work Order - Transfer Posting
entry.content:
Hello Preethi,

The first question is - so the object is physically at your Vendor's location, right?
Did you reflect it in your SAP system already? 
It means you do the Transfer Posting from your Stock to Subcontracting Stock - with the 'O' indicator, which you noticed in your error.
You can do it fully manually via MIGO, or launch ADSUBCON and do it there.
The to be refurbished object has to be on the 'O' stock, with valuation type DAMAGED (or whatever it's called in your system).

Then you receive the refurbished object back, again via ADSUBCON or via MIGO.
Let me know if you solved it. 

Cheers,
Dan

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud Integration API - Collection of OData Clients using JS/TS and SAP Cloud SDK
entry.content: @Willem_PardaensTy so much for the feedbacks and support, I've updated the repository README.md!

entry.title: Re: Quota / Incorrect planning figures
entry.content:
Hello

You have a base quantity allocated for supplier 1225337, so the quota arrangement is creating more proposals for vendor 132250 to adjust the allocated quantity according to the quotas. 

Regards,
Caetano

There is just a small typo (MS PowerPoint, not MS Project)

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Business Partner – Address Management at Business User
entry.content:
Thanks, @Andi_M , this is helpful. And maybe @Hemker_B can chime in.

However, it's also disturbing. Our IAM/IdP user provisioning system is providing the Last Name, Full Name, Display Name, NickName, Alias, etc of the SAP User object (not HCM (Employee object), not 1 or 2 BuPa objects (Natural Person). It's fine if there is a link between BuPa or Employee to User, but that's it: it's just a link. No "ownership" of User Attributes. 

What are options so that our External IAM can control the Last Name, First Name etc (the "black bordered area" in your very clear screenshot) of the SAP User / Identity / Principal (Business User)? (I also see weird behavior when getting "service user" (with no HCM / natural person link) details, but that should be directed to SAP Support / OSS.)

Is there customizing somewhere like TBZ_V_EEWA_SRC? Or is our only option to sever the BuPa-User connection by removing the link in table USR21?

entry.title: Re: $filter with 'and' / 'or' options for select query
entry.content: I know this is a very old thread, but in case anyone stumbles across it looking for an answer. This is still a limitation in the Gateway. For my particular scenario, I was able to get around it by using NE/AND to exclude the values I was not interested in instead of EQ/OR.

entry.title: Re: Refurbishment Work Order - Transfer Posting
entry.content: You can use ME2ON or ME2O - plus if you are using MIGO with a purchase order -- make sure the movement type on the right-hand side in 541--No other need to mention anything and the system should be able to take to move the stock to vendor (MBLB)

entry.title: How can I open one PM plan with two different equipment that have different cycle start date
entry.content:
How can I open one PM plan with two different equipment that have different cycle start date


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How can I open one PM plan with two different equipme...
entry.content: You can add two maintenance items - one for each equipment. Both these items will have the same cycle. It will not be possible to have different cycle start dates.

entry.title: Net due date calculation with Working Days only
entry.content:
We need to calculate the Net due date according to payment terms with skipping the non-working days (like Saturday / Sunday).

In short system should consider only working days during calculation of Net due date.

In Sap generally system calculate all days but we need to Skip the Saturday, Sunday & non working days during calculation of Net Due Date.

We did not find BADI or Public Cloud SAP Notes that could be used in an extensibility to meet this requirement.

Please, could you please guide in this regard, since i went through the many thread but there were not any concrete resolution on it.

entry.title: Re: Prevent billing doc creation when vbrk & vbrp rec...
entry.content: we need to set billing block in sales order after checking XVBRK, XVBRP,XKOMV tables record for a delivery where pricing is within decided margin so we are able to achieve that part using the user exit - USEREXIT_FILL_VBRK_VBRP in include

entry.title: Re: Prevent billing doc creation when vbrk & vbrp rec...
entry.content: RV60AFZC

entry.title: SAP Digital Manufacturing error invalid equipment indicator association
entry.content:
Hi,

I have a Production Process where I use the Last Indicator Value object, but when I run it I get the following error:

The Production Process is configured and runs as follows:

 

The asset is configured and linked to an OPC UA connection as follows:

 

When checking the Production Process Monitor the following message appears:


{"errorCode":"HTTP400","errorMessage":"{\"message\":\"Invalid payload\",\"causeMessage\":\"Invalid payload. [Invalid equipment indicator association]\",\"code\":\"400\",\"correlationId\":\"44b74cd5-6a28-4772-665f-d301f6847c57\"}"}

Do you have any idea why this error can occur?

Thank you very much in advance for your help.
Best Regards!

entry.title: Re: Prevent billing doc creation when vbrk & vbrp rec...
entry.content: But that doesn't stop billing doc creation even if we set the fields vbak-faksk = 'Z4'. and vbap-faksp = 'Z4'.

entry.title: Re: B4HANA SQL Script Execution
entry.content:
From GUI, DB02 is the only option. Alternative, you have to navigate from HANA studio/ Eclipse to execute SQL queries directly against the HANA database.

In SAP HANA Studio/ Eclipse: Navigate to the SQL Console by right-clicking on your system connection and selecting "Open SQL Console."

In SAP HANA Database Explorer: Open SAP HANA Database Explorer through the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) or the SAP HANA Cloud platform. Connect to your HANA instance. Use the SQL console to run your SQL queries.

entry.title: cms calendar
entry.content:
Hello,

We have a custom 2nd Business Day CMS calendar for our monthly reports. The Labor Day holiday threw the schedule off by a day; our monthly reports will run tomorrow, 9/4, instead of today, 9/3. I tried to get them to run today and changed the scheduled date on the CMS calendar from 9/4 to 9/3. This didn't work, so I changed the scheduled date back to tomorrrow, 9/4.  However - our reports run on a trigger event. My question is: will reports need their recurrence changed in order for the event to get triggered overnight tonight?  I hope this is not the case as there are over 200 monthly reports.  The recurrence on these reports is showing as updated as of this morning, when I changed the calendar.

The trigger file gets replaced at around 4:30AM.

Attached are screenshots of one recurrence.

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: What’s New for SAP Integration Suite – August 2024
entry.content: Do we have an option of assigning a proprietary message from a Trading Partner in TPM in Type System under B2B Scenario? I only see the pre-defined options from the drop-down.

entry.title: Re: Prevent billing doc creation when vbrk & vbrp rec...
entry.content: And then populate XVBFS for error endling. PERFORM VBFS_HINZUFUEGEN USING LIPS-POSNR '065' VBAP-VBELN VBAP-POSNR SPACE. SY-SUBRC = 4. EXIT.

entry.title: Re: Prevent billing doc creation when vbrk & vbrp rec...
entry.content: we need a solution to stop billing doc creation after xvbrk xvbrp structures are filled to do validation when these tables are filled we are able to do validation but unsuccessful in stopping the billing doc creation. We need the solution for background run using VF06, VF04 so that next billing doc creation is not interrupted if the previous billing doc is not created and we are dealing with delivery related billing doc creation.

entry.title: Re: How to access Employee central report and ECP reports in BTP
entry.content: Hi Sowmi, Can you connect BTP with ECP directly?

entry.title: Re: Understanding SuccessFactors Pay Scale Structure and its related features
entry.content:
Hi MicheleCordano,

I haven't had to do it. I haven't tested but my first instinct would be to make sure that the user doing the Tarif change have the permission in Employee Central Import Settings to enable rule on import for comp info / job info. Let me know if it works, it will save me time when I get the requirements!

Cheers,
Stéphanie

entry.title: Re: Return to supplier process in case of replaced Goods
entry.content:
Hi,

If the material master already exists and it is just new batch or characteristics then you just need to update the Material master for the Characteristics in Classification view of MM. For batch you have two options one you can create the new batch while doing GR or can create through a t code MSC1N.

Thanks,

Sanan

entry.title: Re: SAP Digital Manufacturing error invalid equipment ind...
entry.content:
Have you tried removing "/" from 'structurePath' or omitting 'structurePath' mapping completely?
I suggest you check this detailed blog post: Last Indicator Value in Line Monitor POD within SAP Digital Manufacturing and review your setup.
Hope it helps,
Manoel

entry.title: Re: To stop Email notifications related to workitems
entry.content:
Hi,

If you have the program name just search in SM37 what background job is sending those emails and there you can edit the Variant and can remove those two people name from it. Also, please check SOST which may give you more idea.

Thanks,

Sanan

Will you attend TechEd? Please consider joining SAP TechEd - SAP Community?

If you are wondering what sessions to attend you can also check out the Not sure which sessions to attend? We asked some S... - SAP Community

Best of luck!

entry.title: how long will REA be maintained/supported by SAP ?
entry.content: Can anyone tell me how long that REA-functionality will be maintained/supported by SAP ? ...for some reason I did not find it on the roadmap explorer. Thx a lot !

entry.title: Re: Task 3 - Enhancing CDS View Entities with Annotations
entry.content: Here's the revised version.

entry.title: Re: Last Indicator Value in Line Monitor POD within SAP Digital Manufacturing
entry.content: Hello @jppalominos , as described in the blog, you can either use Automatic Triggers of Subscription type to monitor each tag value changes in the OPC server and call this Production Process sending the latest tag values as input to 'inValue' for each indicator, or you can use an Automatic Triggers of Timer type to call a Production Process every X interval that reads the current tag values and calls Last Indicator Value for each indicator. Hope it helps, Manoel

entry.title: Using Default Route transit time in the Freight Unit scheduling
entry.content:
Hello TM experts,

One of the TM master data that is frequently used in our customers process is the Default Route. It helps us correctly generate the freight stages when additional stages need to be considered during a freight execution. For example, in an export scenario, if we have to consider road transport to the port of origin, sea freight to the port of destination and also the last road leg to the end customer, it is the default route that helps us specify the correct breakdown of these stages.

One of the features of the Default Route is that we can register the transit time of each stage and during the creation of the transport documents, this time is considered to calculate the departure and arrival dates of each stop. When we use a process coming from a FWO, this use is clearer. However, I often see doubts about how to use this when creating the TOR documents. This blog aims to provide a step-by-step guide on what needs to be configured for this to work. So let's go step by step:

1) Create your default route specifying your transit time:

2) Make your Freight Unit Building Rule consider the application of default route:

3) Here's the golden tip: Just having the transit time registered in the default route does not tell the system the correct way to use it. To do this, we need to create a planning profile with a scheduling strategy to be considered. Here I used the strategy without optimization (VSS_EMBED), calculating the times from the arrival date backwards. Don't forget to register the planning horizon in the selection profile:

4) Add this planning profile to your freight unit configuration. Additionally, enable automatic DDD calculation. When creating a FU where DDD calculation is enabled, the TM checks if there is a default route being used and a registered planning profile to then perform the calculation. If there is no planning profile, it proceeds to call the DDD settings. If it is not configured, it does not perform this calculation:

5) Make the other logistics integration settings so that your document integrates correctly with the TM and uses your created FUBR (The settings may vary if your implementation is embedded or side-car, but they are not the focus of this process).

6) Let's test it!

(Screenshot from internal SAP environments)

Feel free to leave your comments and suggestions.

entry.title: Re: workflows approvers automatic update
entry.content:
Hi Jerry, I think this is a huge win for you when you implement this. Basically in any workflow, you can create a method to look for the necessary approvers prior to the actual approval task. You can also define a Rule (PFAC) to locate your approvers, but over time, I have found that I prefer to have the 1 - 'Get Approvers' task immediately preceding the 2 - 'Do Approval' and pass the results from  step 1 to step 2 using the WF container.

These approvers can be found in the Org Structure (preferred) or in a table.   You can even use the name of the person who created or changed an object, and find their 'boss' via the Org Structure.

We look in the Org Structure for almost all of our approvers.  They may be identified by their position, job, assignment to a custom SAP Org Object, or a relationship from their position to an organization.  As with many things in SAP, there are multiple ways to do this.  For example, if you decide that your Vendor Invoice Approvers need to hold a custom relationship to the organization on the receiving end of the invoice - in my world, we can always navigate from the main cost center to a specific org unit, so we were able to define a custom relationship linking Invoice Approvers to the org unit(s) incurring the costs.

Then you can write a FM or method to look for that Org Unit, and find the positions that hold that custom relationship.  You would execute this method prior to your approval task, and bind the results into the WF container, and then have your approval task be delivered to the agents in that multiline container element.

Using the HR Org structure has some built-in benefits - mostly that the people must be actively employed.  If you can do that, then I'll point you to RH_STRUC_GET as a FM that will be useful to you.

You can also attach SAP Org Objects to people/positions/jobs and look up the holders via table HRP1208.  For more on this approach, see https://help.sap.com/doc/saphelp_nw73ehp1/7.31.19/en-US/4e/ef52aa451757dde10000000a421bc1/content.htm?no_cache=true

Good luck!

Sue

entry.title: Re: SAP Purchase requisition APP - scale pricing not show...
entry.content:
Hi,

There is no link between PIR and PR. You can maintain price scale at PIR but it will only be visible when you create a PO. At PR level PIR data will not be visible.

Thanks,

Sanan

entry.title: Re: Decision Key Not showing In My Inbox after Re-work Pu...
entry.content:
Hi,

Are you using Flexable workflow?

Please check the below Note:

2421360 - Action Buttons are not displayed in Fiori My inbox App - SAP for Me

Thanks,

Sanan

entry.title: Re: VLMS On Mobile
entry.content:
Hello @BijuG,

Please see available SAP SuccessFactors Apps. There are three SAP SuccessFactors apps available for iOS mobile devices. The titles are listed as they appear in the Apple App Store.

  • SAP Validated Learning

  • SAP Validated Learning Preview

  • SAP SuccessFactors Mobile

And for standard supported features, please see SAP SuccessFactors Mobile Features

See Also: 2736082 - SuccessFactors Learning Administration is not supported on smartphone browsers

Thank you!

entry.title: Re: Favorite book and SAP Community Readers?
entry.content:
Great questions.  Sometimes I will read parts of a book and skip chapters - for example, any book about the Kennedys - I'll skip chapters.  I don't think I simply stopped reading a book.

I do read multiple books at the same time - I am usually reading a fiction, biography and self help books all at the same time!

entry.title: Re: SAP Purchase requisition APP - scale pricing not show...
entry.content:
PR does not have scale pricing -nor it make sense from a business point of view. If your PIR is the source of supply, the valuation price would be based on the material master valuation. At the time of Ordering, Scales price was adopted in the PO. 

In Short, PR there is no scale pricing. PIR scale pricing is only available via WebGui T-codes-- SAP S4 manage purchasing info records does not have that functionality or does not allow or display scale pricing.

entry.title: Re: Using NFC or similar contactless technology for labor...
entry.content:
Hi,

Sure! I would expect that a NFC reader or a barcode scanner would work just fine as input methods, just like a regular keyboard for entering the badge information. I mean, if the focus is on the badge field, I would expect that a locally connected NFC reader or barcode scanner would just read the data and drop it in the badge field.

In any case, if you need anything different, you can always consider custom POD plugins and Time Tracking APIs, see: https://api.sap.com/api/sapdme_timetracking/resource/Attendance

Hope it helps,
Manoel

entry.title: Re: Task 4 - Implement Criticality feature and create an Object page
entry.content: Task 4:

entry.title: Re: Using Badges for Labor Tracking in SAP Digital Manufacturing
entry.content:
Hello @OliverQB ,

Sure! I would expect that a NFC reader or a barcode scanner would work just fine as input methods, just like a regular keyboard for entering the badge information. I mean, if the focus is on the badge field, I would expect that a locally connected NFC reader or barcode scanner would just read the data and drop it in the badge field.

In any case, if you need anything different, you can always consider custom POD plugins and Time Tracking APIs, see: https://api.sap.com/api/sapdme_timetracking/resource/Attendance

Hope it helps,
Manoel

entry.title: Re: Return to supplier process in case of replaced Goods
entry.content:
1. Return PO means -- You have 161--then Credit memo to be posted -- You may have done the first 2 steps --Now if the vendor is supplying the materials as a replacement -- only option is to create a new PO or 2nd line item on the same PO as the same amount as created return PO-- then perform 101 with new characteristics -- and post the invoice as the same amount as credit memo

You could save yourself from posting credit memos and invoices if you have checked the free indicator for both lines while creating the PO and before posting 161.

entry.title: Re: 7 Most Commonly Asked Questions About Package Configuration Manager for SAP SuccessFactors Solut
entry.content:
Now that the OpenSAP courses have been sunset, can someone update the links/references to the OpenSAP with their corresponding LearningHub URLs/course names?

Thanks
Chandra

entry.title: Understanding the Source of 'To Do List' Dates in SAP CATS Classic Fiori App
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I am currently configuring the "My Time Sheet" app in SAP Fiori for use with CATS Classic. I've successfully set up employees in both the HR Mini Master and PPOME. Within the app, there is a "To Do List" tab that displays all the days for which time has not been reported.

I would like to understand the logic behind how the system determines which dates appear in this "To Do List." Specifically, from which date does the system start tracking the days without time entries? Is this based on the employee's start date, or is there another date that the system uses to begin counting?

Any insights or explanations on how the "To Do List" dates are generated would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you in advance for your assistance.

entry.title: Re: Meas doc creation allows points for items not related...
entry.content: As far my understanding goes -you have to enforce the use of measurement points belonging to FL or Equipment in the notification using the BADI only -- but make sure to perform negative testing thoroughly for entry list or individual measurement point entry as well.

entry.title: Re: Using Badges for Labor Tracking in SAP Digital Manufacturing
entry.content:
Hello @UtkarshRaj , 

Yes! When you approve time records for direct labor that contain a standard value, the duration is transferred to integrated ERP systems. Learn more at: https://help.sap.com/docs/sap-digital-manufacturing/execution/labor-tracking

The flag you enabled ('Report Duration of Production Time to ERP') does not automatically report time back to ERP, 'Enable Auto-Approval for Time Records' should do it. Learn more about time tracking flags at: https://help.sap.com/docs/sap-digital-manufacturing/execution/manage-business-settings

Hope it helps,
Manoel
